notes: Resume-session eval passes 100% in both configurations — assertions may not be discriminating enough for this test case. Consider adding assertions for skill-specific behaviors (subagent dispatch, worktree isolation, structured handoff notes format).
The skill's unique value is in worktree isolation, subagent dispatch, and pre-flight checks — none of which the baseline knows about.
Without timing/token data (simulated runs), time and token comparisons are not meaningful.
The baseline performs surprisingly well on mode behavior (semi-auto, auto, unattended) — likely because ohno's MCP tool names are descriptive enough for Claude to infer correct behavior.
